title: torch-npu requires PyYAML as undeclared transitive dependency
problem_description: torch-npu 2.5.1's memory visualization submodule (_memory_viz.py) imports yaml unconditionally, but PyYAML is not declared in the package's pip metadata. When creating a fresh venv, torch import fails with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'yaml' during torch_npu backend loading, cascading into complete torch import failure.
rough_fix_approach: Pre-install PyYAML in the venv before using torch-npu: pip install PyYAML -i https://mirrors.aliyun.com/pypi/simple/. Alternatively, pin requirements.txt to include PyYAML alongside torch and torch-npu.
artifact_evidence: validated/phase_5_validation_canonical.json: loop_state.last_error_signature full traceback at torch_npu/npu/_memory_viz.py line 11
raw/phase_5_validation_attempt0.json: same error trace in iteration 1, resolved by iteration 2

involved_code_files.role: torch-npu internal — memory visualization module with unconditional yaml import
torch-npu internal — re-exports from _memory_viz, triggers yaml import chain

reasoning: This affects every CUDA-to-NPU migration that creates a fresh venv with torch-npu. The error is misleading (traceback points to torch import failure, masking the real missing yaml module), so pre-installing PyYAML saves significant debugging time. Verified on CANN 8.1.RC1 with torch-npu 2.5.1.
